preamble: This phonetic transformation, from a voiced bilabial plosive [b] to a voiceless velar plosive [k], is considered highly unusual and a rarity in historical linguistics, as it involves both devoicing and a significant shift in the place of articulation. Despite its 'unnatural' appearance as a single step, it is a well-documented historical sound change in the Berawan language.

examples.note: In Berawan dialects, Proto-North Sarawak (PNS) or earlier *b shifted to k in intervocalic position. While this appears as a direct shift, some analyses propose it as a telescoping of intermediate, more phonetically natural changes, such as *b > g (velarization) followed by g > k (intervocalic devoicing).

sources: Blust, Robert. 2025. *b > -k-: A Berawan sound change for the ages. In Natalia Kuznetsova, Cormac Anderson & Shelece Easterday (eds.), Rarities in phonetics and phonology: Structural, typological, evolutionary, and social dimensions, 87–113.
Beguš, Gašper & Robert Blust. 2024. The blurring history of intervocalic devoicing. Journal of Linguistics.
